data.body:
Implemented ONE shared ref-resolution boundary attest._hash_basis(repo_root, *, pinned_sha) that BOTH plan-review hashing (dependency_hashes/_rehash) AND the claim-gate freshness re-check (claim_gate_check) resolve through. Plan-review records the snapshot SHA as a verified-at-sha manifest step (via S4's signing channel + current_code_sha() = the content-addressed snapshot dir name); the claim gate reads that pin back out and re-hashes file_impact at the SAME SHA's snapshot — so a working-tree drift can no longer cause a false stale-code (the divergence ADR 0002 guards). Resolution order: pinned_sha (materialize snapshot, fetch=False) > current_code_root (active attested snapshot) > working tree. AC1 one boundary + same pinned-SHA basis; AC2 negative/divergence test (sign attested, drift the working tree, assert NOT stale-code); AC3 ADR 0002 updated with the consolidation; AC4 back-out (local/legacy attestation has no pin -> both sides fall back to the working tree, exactly the prior per-site behavior; reverting is a no-op rollback).

Subagent review: NO blocking issues, all 4 ACs verified met; the two pinned SHAs are provably identical (current_code_sha = basename of the snapshot entry dir = the SHA the claim gate re-acquires). Non-blocking (design-consistent, noted): the UNSCOPED whole-HEAD fallback path is unchanged (out of scope); a cold-cache claim degrades fail-closed (conservative). Conftest defaults gates to source=local so existing plan-review tests keep working-tree hashing (unaffected). 4 S4b tests + 122 plan-review/claim-gate tests green; ruff clean.
